preview_text:
The model rates Colombia as a strong favorite with a 75% win probability, leaving DR Congo with only a 4% chance of victory. The 71-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial projected disparity in match quality on a neutral ground.

The outcome distribution suggests Colombia enters as clear favorites, but the 20% draw probability indicates the match carries genuine uncertainty. A stalemate remains a plausible result, and while a DR Congo upset ranks as unlikely under the model's assessment, the non-trivial draw chance prevents the fixture from appearing one-sided in practical terms.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully. Bookmakers assign Colombia 64.6% and DR Congo 12.9%—roughly 11 and 8 percentage points higher than the model's respective figures—while draw odds align closely across sources. Polymarket prices track the consensus. The dashboard does not claim superiority over market pricing; the gap warrants monitoring to identify which framework better calibrates the true competitive balance.
